{{Quote|The Academy at Sab Rufo, from which Rin claims to hold two doctoral degrees (and at which he claims to be the current Director of Sentientology), is nonexistent. In fact, Sab Rufo itself is nonexistent.|Major Herrit, Imperial communique #46553.27h|<ref name="GG4" />}}
'''Sab Rufo''' was an [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] astronomical object that was the site of the [[Academy at Sab Rufo]]. At one point during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Herrit]] of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] sent a [[Imperial Communique 46553.27h|communique]], addressed to [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], in which they erroneously claimed that neither the academy nor Sab Rufo itself existed. In response, Vader's [[Aide/Legends|aide]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Solistein]] claimed that the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] himself had visited Sab Rufo.

==Description==
Sab Rufo was a celestial body<ref name="GG4" /> located in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim's]] [[Sab Rufo system/Legends|Sab Rufo system]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
[[File:DarthVader-Hasbro.jpg|thumb|right|250px|According to Captain Solistein, Darth Vader (pictured) at some point visited Sab Rufo.]]
Sab Rufo was the site of the [[Academy at Sab Rufo]], at which the [[sentientologist]] [[Obo Rin]] claimed to hold the position of [[Director of Sentientology]]. At some point by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Herrit]] of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] performed a background check on Rin, whom the Empire had commissioned to [[Author/Legends|author]] a [[Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy|reference work]] detailing the various [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The major subsequently sent [[Imperial Communique 46553.27h|communique #46553.27h]], addressed to<ref name="GG4" /> the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]<ref name="CSWE Vader">{{CSWECite|III|288|Vader, Darth}}</ref> [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], in which Herrit criticized Rin and<ref name="GG4" /> erroneously<ref name="Planets">''[[The Star Wars Planets Collection]]''</ref> claimed that the Academy at Sab Rufo—or indeed Sab Rufo itself—did not in fact exist.<ref name="GG4" />

In a [[Imperial Communique 46553.28v|reply communique]], Vader's [[Aide/Legends|aide]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Solistein]] dismissed Herrit's "unsolicited opinions" and, in response to the latter's criticisms of Rin's qualifications, stated that Vader had not ordered Herrit to perform a background check on the sentientologist. In addition, Solistein noted that Vader himself had visited Sab Rufo.<ref name="GG4" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:FringeTerritories-Voyages13.png|thumb|right|200px|Map depicting Sab Rufo that was published in ''Voyages SF'' 13.]]
Sab Rufo was introduced in ''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]'', a [[1989]] sourcebook authored by [[Troy Denning]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="GG4" /> "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, placed Sab Rufo, as well as<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Challenge">{{ChallengeCite|37|Wookiees Amok}}</ref> [[Branth]] and the [[Colu system|Colu]], [[Endor system/Legends|Endor]], [[Harridan system/Legends|Harridan]], [[New Bakstre system/Legends|New Bakstre]], and [[Solaest system/Legends|Solaest systems]], in the [[Fringe Territories]].<ref name="Twinkle" /> The adventure is considered [[Canon|noncanonical]] with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="Unlicensed">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Sab Rufo [[Star system/Legends|system]], and thereby Sab Rufo itself, in grid square N-8.<ref name="TEA" />
